text: 2007 is installed in one virtual machine and Microsoft Word is installed in another machine So what you can do here is the same thing in virtual environment You make a virtual environment in which all your packages are there and that means it is not from your global interpreter Means your python installation, the python we have installed, a copy of that installation will be made And in that copy we will install different versions of the package and the main python of that copy will not make any sense I hope I could explain this thing We will be able to make an isolated environment So what we have to do here is getting requirements to build wheel, it is still running And I guess it is running So I will wait for it, it will be completed or I can cancel it if I want And I will directly install virtualenv here virtualenv, so virtualenv is a package that is being installed now, you can see virtualenv has been installed Now I will write virtualenv here and I will name it env I can name my environment anything, I have kept env here, it is asking me that you have created a new environment Do you want to select it for this folder, I will say yes I want to do it, so I have done yes here So you can see here it is showing me that a new virtual environment has been created using 3.1, 2.3 And a folder will be created here, you can see a folder named env has been created In which there is lib, scripts, basically a python isolated environment has been created here Which you can use and you can put any version in it Now what I will do is I will come here where these two files are made, you can see our python file is made and an env folder is created in which our environment is I will shift right and open in terminal and you can see what I can do here I will activate this virtual environment, so if I write here pip install pandas And I hit enter, so you can see the requirement is already satisfied here, it is telling me that look Pandas are already installed in your computer, if I do equals to equals to 1.5.2 If I want to install 1.5.2 pandas, then it will download 1.5.2 and install it in my global python installation But now I want to work in 1.5.1 or any lower version So for that I will install that pandas in this environment, I will show you how we can do this So I let it finish and once it is finished, then we will activate this virtual environment So you see here that once we install the virtual environment package and after that if we make a new virtual environment Then we can activate that virtual environment, I will tell you how to activate that virtual environment What is a virtual environment? It is an environment which is same as a system interpreter but is isolated from the other python environments on the system So the python you have installed, it becomes a different copy of your python installation, so let's see if it is finished or not It is still building the wheel, so guys it really took a while, it took a little time and here you can see that it has uninstalled Pandas 2.2.2 and it is installing 1.5.2, now what I will do here, I will activate this virtual environment and to activate it I will simply type here .slashenv, I will press tab and I will press scripts and I will type activate.ps1 and I will press enter And you see here in bracket it is writing env which means that the environment is activated Now when I am running VS code, then this env will not come in the bracket but because I have already done venv here So all my packages will be installed in the virtual environment, so you have to remember this thing, env does not show VS code here Now I will come back here and I will install any pip, let's say I have installed pip here and I have written pandas here and I have hit enter So what will happen is that the latest version of pandas 2.2.2 will be installed here and here you can see 1.5.2 which is installed in my global So I am installing it here in the virtual environment and if I import pandas and I print the version of pandas, then you see how I can print it So I am waiting for it to be installed and if I write python here and I write import pandas and I write pandas.__version__, then you see 2.2.2 is showing because I am in the virtual environment Now if I write exit here and I run exit and I write exit again which will make me exit from this terminal itself, now I can write deactivate and activate the virtual environment Now if I write python here and I write import pandas, you saw that 2.2.2 was there earlier but if I write pandas.__version__, then 1.5.2 is showing which means I can install different versions of pandas in my global python and in this virtual environment I hope that makes sense, now we will come back here and we will listen about pip freeze command, now if I want to know which packages are installed in my python installation, then I will run pip freeze command Now if I write pip freeze, then it will list all the packages, as you can see my virtual environment is already active because whenever you activate the virtual environment in escode, then your env is not shown in the bracket Now if I open the terminal and I run pip freeze, then you can see the pandas version is shown here and I have installed many more packages, pandas where are you? I know you can see pandas but I can't see it, 1.5.2 or 2.2.2, this is my virtual environment and this is my global python Now if I want to write this in a file, then I can run this command pip freeze requirement.txt, it's a good practice that we take all our important packages in requirements.txt file, so as soon as I hit enter, you can see requirements.txt is created, now to recreate this virtual environment, I have to write pip install-r requirements.txt Now if I hit enter, then all these packages will be installed one by one in any virtual environment, so whenever we make a python project, then we run pip freeze command and make the requirements.txt file, the benefit of this is that all the python packages are stored in the requirements.txt file and then using that information, we can recreate that environment by writing pip install-r requirements.txt Now let's see about lambda function, lambda functions are some functions which we make using the help of lambda keyword, I want to show you that if I want to make a function called square, then I can make it in two ways, I make a function here by the name of 0 2 underscore lambda.py So I can say that square is a function which takes n and returns n into n and if I print square of 5, then the square of 5 will be printed here, but I can also make it like a variable by doing something like this, I can say that square is equal to lambda which will take x and return x into x and if I run this, then you will see that it will also give 25, so lambda function is a way to create a function using an expression, so function is in the form of an expression, like you see here square is equal to lambda x colon x star x is written, so here we can also make it by giving lambda and arguments, so I have given a comma b comma c and return a plus b plus c, so in a way this is a shortcut to make functions in a single line and to make functions in a single line, so you can make a variable which is a function, so guys the next topic is join method which is a very simple thing, I will show you how the join method works, so the join method works for strings and what you have to do is that suppose I have a string and I have a string called a is equal to Harry, and let us say I have a list, I have a string list, I have Harry and I have Rohan and let's keep one more Shubham in it, now if I want to join these three with a dash, then
